__Array.summary:
Internal type that implements the VO Compatible ARRAY type.
This type has methods and properties that normally are never directly called from user code.

__Array.__GetElement.returns: The element stored at the specified location in the array.

__Array.__SetElement.returns: The element stored at the specified location in the array.

__Array.ctor.summary: Create an array and fill it with elements from an existing .Net array of USUALS

__Array.DefaultValue.summary: Returns the default value for array elements when arrays are resized or initialized. This is NIL.

__Array.IMPLICIT.summary: Implicit conversion to OBJECT[]. SubArrays become nested OBJECT[] arrays.

__Array.SELF_index.returns: The value of the property of the element stored at the indicated location in the array.

__Array.SELF_indices.returns: The value of the property of the element stored at the indicated location in the array.

__ArrayBase_T_.summary:
Internal type that implements the new TYPED ARRAY type.
This type has methods and properties that normally are never directly called from user code.
__ArrayBase_T_.typeparam: Type of the elements inside the array

__ArrayBase_T_.__GetElement.returns: The element stored at the specified location in the array.

__ArrayBase_T_.__SetElement.param: New element to store in the array at the position specified

__ArrayBase_T_.ctor_2.summary: Create an array with a certain capacity.

__ArrayBase_T_.ctor_3.summary: Create an array with a certain capacity and specify if it should be filled with default values.

__ArrayBase_T_.ctor_4.summary: Create an array and fill it with elements from an existing collection.

__ArrayBase_T_.ctor_5.summary: Create an array and fill it with elements from an existing .Net array of objects. Note that the objects must be of the right type.

__ArrayBase_T_.ctor_6.summary: Create an array and fill it with elements from an existing .Net array.

__ArrayBase_T_.DefaultValue.summary: Returns the default value for array elements when arrays are resized or initialized.

__ArrayBase_T_.EXPLICIT.summary: Implicitely convert a typed array to an array of USUALs.

__ArrayBase_T_.IMPLICIT.summary: Implicitely convert an array of USUALs to a typed array. Note that the usuals must contain a value of the correct type.

__ArrayBase_T_.IMPLICIT_2.summary: Implicitely convert a typed Array to an OBJECT[].

__ArrayBase_T_.SELF_index.returns: The element stored at the indicated location in the collection.

__Binary.summary:
Internal type that implements the FoxPro Compatible BINARY type.
This type has many operators and implicit converters that normally are never directly called from user code. The data in this type is stored as an array of Bytes
Conversions from and to String are supported and they use the current active windows codepage.

__Binary.ctor.param: Byte[] value that has the bytes that define the binary
STRING that will be converted to bytes using the current windows codepage.

remarks.remarks: When you create a Binary from a string, then accented characters and characters outside the current windows codepage may be replaced with a question mark. and when the current codepage is a so called multi byte codepage than one character may be mapped to more than one byte in the result

__Binary.Value.summary: Binary value as array of Bytes

__Currency.summary: Internal type that implements the FoxPro Compatible CURRENCY type. This type has many operators and implicit converters that normally are never directly called from user code. The data in this type is stored as a System.Decimal with 4 decimal places

__Currency.ctor.param: Real8 value to convert to a CURRENCY
Decimal value to convert to a CURRENCY
Float value to convert to a CURRENCY
ICurrency value to convert to a CURRENCY

__Date.summary:
Internal type that implements the VO Compatible DATE type
This type has many operators and implicit converters that normally are never directly called from user code. It holds year, month, day in 32 bits. For date calculations it uses the System.DateTime calculation logic. There are implicit converters between Date and DateTime.

__Date.ctor.param: DateTime value to convert to a DATE
Date value to convert to a DATE
Ticks value to convert to a DATE
String value to convert to a DATE
Year value to convert to a DATE
Month value to convert to a DATE
Day value to convert to a DATE

__Float.summary: Internal type that implements the XBase Compatible FLOAT type. This type has many operators and implicit converters that normally are never directly called from user code.

__Float.ctor.param: Real8 value to convert to a FLOAT
Number of significant decimals of the FLOAT
IFLoat value to copy into the FLOAT

__Psz.summary:
Internal type that implements the XBase Compatible PSZ type.
This type has many operators and implicit converters that normally are never directly called from user code.

__Symbol.summary:
Internal type that implements the XBase Compatible SYMBOL type.
This type has many operators and implicit converters that normally are never directly called from user code.
There are also some operators that handle implicit conversions between Symbols and Strings in your code.

__Symbol.ctor.param: String value to convert to a SYMBOL
Should the symbol be converted to Uppercase
Position of an existing symbol in the symbol table.

__Usual.summary:
Internal type that implements the XBase Compatible USUAL type.
This type has many operators and implicit converters that normally are never directly called from user code.

__Usual.__InexactEquals.summary: This method is used by the compiler for code that does an inexact comparison between two usuals.
__Usual.__InexactEquals.param: Left hand side of the comparison
Right hand side of the comparison
__Usual.__InexactEquals.returns: TRUE when the values are equal
__Usual.__InexactEquals.remarks: This method takes the SetExact() setting into account when comparing string values.

__Usual.__InexactNotEquals.summary: This method is used by the compiler for code that does an inexact comparison.
__Usual.__InexactNotEquals.param: Left hand side of the comparison
Right hand side of the comparison
__Usual.__InexactNotEquals.returns: TRUE when the values are NOT equal
__Usual.__InexactNotEquals.remarks: This method takes the SetExact() setting into account when comparing string values.

__Usual.CompareTo.summary: This method is needed to implement the IComparable interface.

__Usual.ctor.summary: This constructor is used in code generated by the compiler when needed.
__Usual.ctor.param: object that will be stored inside the usual.
__Usual.ctor.remarks: When the object is a value type, the the value will be stored inside the usual. Otherwise a reference to the value will be stored.

__Usual.IsArray.summary: This property returns TRUE when the USUAL is of type Array

__Usual.IsBinary.summary: This property returns TRUE when the USUAL is of type Binary

__Usual.IsByRef.summary: This property returns TRUE when the USUAL is passed by reference (not implemented yet)

__Usual.IsCodeblock.summary: This property returns TRUE when the USUAL is of type Codeblock

__Usual.IsCurrency.summary: This property returns TRUE when the USUAL is of type Currency

__Usual.IsDate.summary: This property returns TRUE when the USUAL is of type Date

__Usual.IsDateTime.summary: This property returns TRUE when the USUAL is of type DateTime

__Usual.IsDecimal.summary: This property returns TRUE when the USUAL is of type Decimal

__Usual.IsEmpty.summary: This property returns TRUE when the USUAL is Empty.

__Usual.IsFloat.summary: This property returns TRUE when the USUAL is of type FLOAT

__Usual.IsFractional.summary: This property returns TRUE when the USUAL is of type FLOAT, Decimal or Currency

__Usual.IsInt64.summary: This property returns TRUE when the USUAL is of type Int64

__Usual.IsInteger.summary: This property returns TRUE when the USUAL is of type LONG or INT64

__Usual.IsLogic.summary: This property returns TRUE when the USUAL is of type LOGIC

__Usual.IsLong.summary: This property returns TRUE when the USUAL is of type Long

__Usual.IsNil.summary: This property returns TRUE when the USUAL is NIL, or when the usual is a reference type and NULL or when the isual is a PTR type and IntPtr.Zero

__Usual.IsNull.summary: This property returns TRUE when the USUAL is of type NULL

__Usual.IsNumeric.summary: This property returns TRUE when the USUAL is of type LONG, Int64, FLOAT or Decimal

__Usual.IsObject.summary: This property returns TRUE when the USUAL is of type Object

__Usual.IsPsz.summary: This property returns TRUE when the USUAL is of type Psz

__Usual.IsPtr.summary: This property returns TRUE when the USUAL is of type Ptr (IntPtr)

__Usual.IsReferenceType.summary: This property returns TRUE when the USUAL is a reference type (Array, Decimal, Object, String)

__Usual.IsString.summary: This property returns TRUE when the USUAL is of type String

__Usual.IsSymbol.summary: This property returns TRUE when the USUAL is of type Symbol

__Usual.SELF_index.returns: The element stored at the indicated location in the array.
__Usual.SELF_index.remarks: When the contents of the USUAL is not an array or does not support indexed access then a runtime error is generated.

__Usual.SELF_name.returns: The element stored at the indicated location in the collection.
__Usual.SELF_name.remarks: When the contents of the USUAL is not an array or does not name based indexing then a runtime error is generated.

__Usual.SystemType.summary: This property returns the System.Type that represents the value of the usual.

__Usual.Type.summary: This property returns the __UsualType of the USUAL

__Usual.Value.summary: Return the value of the USUAL as object. NIL values are shown as a NIL string.

__UsualEnumerator.summary: Create an enumerator for the USUAL type or throw a runtime exception.
__UsualEnumerator.remarks: This function is automatically called by code generated by the compiler when enumerating an untyped variable

__WinBool.summary: Internal type that implements the WIN32 Compatible LOGIC type in UNIONs and VOSTRUCTs

__WinBool.ctor.summary: This constructor is used in code generated by the compiler when needed.

__WinDate.summary: Internal type that implements the XBase Compatible DATE type in UNIONs and VOSTRUCTs

__WinDate.ctor.summary: This constructor is used in code generated by the compiler when needed.

__WinDate.JulianValue.summary: Value as Julian Number, 0 for NULL_DATE

_ArrayToObjectArray.summary: Helper function to convert ARRAY to OBJECT[]
_ArrayToObjectArray.remarks: This is a helper function used for late bound code that can also be called from user code.

_ArrayToUsualArray.summary: Helper function to convert ARRAY to USUAL[]
_ArrayToUsualArray.remarks: This is a helper function used for late bound code that can also be called from user code.

_CallClipFunc.summary: Call a clipper function by name
_CallClipFunc.param: The name of the function to call.
The list of arguments to pass to the function
The list of arguments to pass to the function
_CallClipFunc.returns: The return value of the function
_CallClipFunc.remarks: Note that X# allows to call functions that are overloaded.

_Codeblock.summary: Internal type that is the base class for macro compiled codeblocks.

_Codeblock.ctor.summary: This constructor is used by the Macro Compiler
_Codeblock.ctor.param: Compiled codeblock created by the macro compiler.
Macro string that was used to create the codeblock.
Did the macro string start with "{|".

_Codeblock.Eval.param: Zero or more arguments to pass to the codeblock.
_Codeblock.Eval.returns: The value of the last expression within the codeblock as a USUAL. If the last expression in the codeblock is of type VOID, then the codeblock returns NIL.

_Codeblock.IsBlock.summary: Was the codeblock created from a string that started with "{|"

_Codeblock.ToString.summary: Returns the original string that was used to create the macro compiled codeblock.

_MClear.summary: Clear all memory variables (all public variables and the private variables of the current thread)

doc._MRelease:
Release variables that match a certain wildcard pattern
The wildcard pattern to use when releasing the memvars. May contain * and ? characters. Indicates if the variables that need to be released should match (TRUE) or NOT match (FALSE) the pattern.
For most dialects the variables are not removed but their values are replaced with NIL.
dsAccessToLocals(TRUE)]; TION _MRelease(cMask AS STRING, lMatch AS LOGIC) AS VOID

_MRestore.summary: Recreate public and private variables previously saved to a file and initialize them with their former values.
_MRestore.param: The name of the memory file to read. The default extension is .MEM
When set to TRUE then the existing memory variables will be saved. Otherwise all memory variables will be deleted first.
(Optional) Wildcard pattern to use when saving. This can include literal characters as well as the standard wildcard characters, * and ?.
(Optional) When set to TRUE then variables matching the pattern are restored. When set to FALSE then the variables that do NOT match the pattern are restored.
_MRestore.remarks: When memory variables are restored, they are recreated as private variables with the scope of the current procedure or function unless they exist as public variables and you specify the ADDITIVE clause . If ADDITIVE is specified, public and private variables with the same names are overwritten unless hidden with PRIVATE. If ADDITIVE is not specified, all public and private variables are released before the memory file is loaded. In that case all restored variables will become private variables.

_MSave.summary: Copy public and private memory variables visible within the current routine to a disk file.
_MSave.param: The name of the file, including an optional drive, directory, and extension. The default extension is .MEM
Wildcard pattern to use when saving. This can include literal characters as well as the standard wildcard characters, * and ?.
When set to TRUE then variables matching the pattern are saved. When set to FALSE then the variables that do NOT match the pattern are saved.
_MSave.remarks: An existing MEM file will be overwritten. When an existing file is ReadOnly then an exception will be thrown.

_MxRelease.summary: Release one or more memory variables variables.
_MxRelease.remarks: The variables are not removed but their values are replaced with NIL.

_ObjectArrayToUsualArray.summary: Helper function to convert OBJECT[] to USUAL[]
_ObjectArrayToUsualArray.remarks: This is a helper function used for late bound code that can also be called from user code.

_SequenceError.summary: This function is automatically inserted by the compiler in a RECOVER USING block and gets called when the RECOVER USING block is reached because of an exception.
_SequenceError.param: The exception that triggered the jump into the RECOVER USING block
_SequenceError.remarks:
The default implementation of this function (in the XSharp.RT assembly) called the installed error handler that is installed with ErrorBlock() The function should then have the following prototype
FUNCTION _SequenceError(e as Exception) AS VOID
_SequenceError.returns: The result of the call to the error handler installed in the ErrorBlock

_SequenceRecover.summary: This function is automatically inserted by the compiler in a compiler generated RECOVER USING block when you have a BEGIN SEQUENCE .. END SEQUENCE in your code without RECOVER USING clause
_SequenceRecover.param: The parameter that was passed in the BREAK statement or the call to the _Break function
_SequenceRecover.remarks:
If a REAL exception occurs then this function is NOT called. The function is only called when the (generated) RECOVER USING block is called with a value from a BREAK statement.
The default implementation of this function (in the XSharp.RT assembly) does nothing. You can override this function in your own code if you want. The function should then have the following prototype
FUNCTION _SequenceRecover(u as USUAL) AS VOID

_Str.returns: The returned string with always have a DOT as decimal separator.

_Str3.returns: A string with DOT as decimal separator.

_TextRestore.summary: This function is used by the TEXT TO FILE UDC

_TextSave.summary: This function is used by the TEXT TO FILE UDC
_TextSave.param: FileName specified in the TEXT TO FILE UDC

_UsualArrayToObjectArray.summary: Helper function to convert USUAL[] to OBJECT[]
_UsualArrayToObjectArray.remarks: This is a helper function used for late bound code that can also be called from user code.

AAdd_T_.typeparam: The type of the array elements

AClone_T_.typeparam: The type of the array elements

ACloneShallow_T_.typeparam: The type of the array elements

ADel_T_.typeparam: The type of the array elements

ADim.summary: Calculate the # of dimensions in an array
ADim.returns: Number of dimensions in an array

ADimPic.summary: Calculate a string that represents the dimensions in an array
ADimPic.returns: String that displays the dimensions in an array

AEval_T_.typeparam: The type of the array elements

AEvalA_T_.typeparam: The type of the array elements

AIns_T_.typeparam: The type of the array elements

ALen_T_.typeparam: The type of the array elements

ArrayCreate_T_.typeparam: The type of the array elements

ArrayDeProtect_T_.typeparam: The type of the array elements

ArrayGet_T_.typeparam: The type of the array elements

ArrayNew_T_.typeparam: The type of the array elements

ArrayProtect_T_.typeparam: The type of the array elements

ArrayPut_T_.typeparam: The type of the array elements

ArrayStore_T_.typeparam: The type of the array elements

ArraySwap_T_.typeparam: The type of the array elements

AScan_T_.typeparam: The type of the array elements
AScan_T_.param: A lambda expression that will be evaluated for every element in the array.

ASize_T_.typeparam: The type of the array elements

ASort_T_.typeparam: The type of the array elements

ATail_T_.typeparam: The type of the array elements

ATrueDel_T_.typeparam: The type of the array elements

BlobData.summary: This class is used to write BLOB data to a FPT file.

CanBreak.summary: Check if a BEGIN SEQUENCE...END construct is active.

Codeblock.summary:
Internal type that implements the VO Compatible CODEBLOCK type
This type has methods that normally are never directly called from user code.

Codeblock.ctor.summary: This constructor is used by the Compiler for compile time codeblocks.
Codeblock.ctor.param: Number of parameters defined in the compile time codeblock.

Codeblock.Eval.param: Zero or more arguments to pass to the codeblock.
Codeblock.Eval.returns: The value of the last expression within the codeblock as a USUAL. If the last expression in the codeblock is of type VOID, then the codeblock returns NIL.
Codeblock.Eval.remarks: This method is abstract and is implemented in the derived classes created by the compiler.

Codeblock.EvalBlock.summary: Eval method that can be called from code that does not "know" about the USUAL type. such as the code in the RDD classes.
Codeblock.EvalBlock.param: Zero or more arguments to pass to the codeblock.

Codeblock.PCount.summary: Returns the number of parameters in the codeblock

Codeblock.ToString.summary: Return a string that contains the # of parameters for display in the debugger.

CompilerServices.summary: This class contains helper code that is called by the compiler to support various XBase language constructs, such as the automatic memory management of PSZ values created with String2Psz().

CompilerServices.CanBreak.summary: Determine if we are inside a BEGIN SEQUENCE .. END by looking at the SEQUENCE counter in the runtime.

CompilerServices.EnterBeginSequence.summary: Increment the SEQUENCE counter for a BEGIN SEQUENCE statement

CompilerServices.ExitBeginSequence.summary: Decrement the SEQUENCE counter for a BEGIN SEQUENCE statement

CompilerServices.String2Psz.summary: Allocate a PSZ and add it to the list

CompilerServices.String2PszRelease.summary: Free all PSZ values in the List

CParamCount.summary: Return the number of arguments that a code block is expecting.

CToD.param: A string representating the date format to use when converting the string to a date. Should consist of D, M and Y characters and separators.

DbAutoLock.summary: Automatically lock a record in the FoxPro dialect

DbAutoLockArea.summary: Automatically lock a record in the FoxPro dialect

DbAutoUnLock.summary: Automatically unlock a record in the FoxPro dialect

DbAutoUnLockArea.summary: Automatically unlock a record in the FoxPro dialect

DbBuffRefresh.summary: Refresh the buffer for the current workarea, discarding any changes that were made.

DbClearScope.summary: Clears the top and/or bottom scope.
DbClearScope.param: An optional constant that indicates which scope needs to be set.
DbClearScope.returns: TRUE when the scope was cleared succesfully, otherwise FALSE.

DbScope.summary: Checks if a scope is set in a work area.
DbScope.param: An optional constant that indicates which scope needs to be set.

DbSetIndex.param: Order in the index file to activate. When not specified then the first order in the file becomes active.

DbSetRelation.param: An optional name for the relation. Defaults to ParentName + "_" + ChildName.

DbSetScope.param: A constant that indicates which scope needs to be set.
The value that needs to be set. The type of the value must match the type of the index expression.
DbSetScope.returns: TRUE when the scope was set succesfully and when the record pointer has been successfully moved to the first record in the scope, otherwise FALSE.
DbSetScope.remarks: The record pointer is moved to the first record in the scope when setting of the scope was succesfull.

DebOut.summary: Write information to the Debug Terminal Program
DebOut.param: List of values to display on the debug terminal

DOY.param: Date for which to calculate the Date

Empty.remarks: For Object values the 'emptyness' is determined by Comparing to NULL_OBJECT and comparign to DBNull.Value

EnableArrayIndexCheck.summary: Suppress Array Index check to be compatible with Visual Objects
EnableArrayIndexCheck.param: TRUE to enable the array index checks.
EnableArrayIndexCheck.returns: The previous setting of the flag
EnableArrayIndexCheck.summary:
In some situations Visual Objects did not throw a runtime error when you were accessing a non existing array element.
You can enable this (mis)behaviour in X# by disabling the array index checks.
The default behavior is to generate an error when you access array indices out of the existing range.

Eval.summary: Evaluate a code block or an objects Eval() method.

FieldGetBytes.summary: Read an array of bytes direct from the workarea buffer.
FieldGetBytes.remarks: This will only work for DBF based workareas (not for Advantage workareas)
FieldGetBytes.returns: The value of the field.` IF does not correspond to the position of any field in the database file, FieldGetBytes() will generate an error.

FieldPutBytes.remarks: This will only work for DBF based workareas (not for Advantage workareas)
FieldPutBytes.summary: Write an array of bytes direct to the workarea buffer.
FieldPutBytes.param: The position OF the FIELD IN the database file structure.
The value to write to the field
FieldPutBytes.returns: The value assigned TO the field. IF does not correspond to the position of any field in the database file, FieldPutBytes() will generate an error.

FindClass.summary: Find a class in the referenced assemblies

FixedMemBlockEnd.summary: Guard Block following MemAlloc return value

FixedMemBlockStart.summary: Guard Block preceding MemAlloc return value

FParamCount.summary: Return the number of local arguments that a function is expecting.
FParamCount.param: The name of the function to examine.
FParamCount.returns: The number of arguments that a method is expecting.
FParamCount.remarks:
Note that you can't use this for functions that are overloaded.
And unlike in VO this function can also be used to return the number of parameters for typed functions.

FReadText3.param: A block of memory to store the data read from the specified file. The length of this variable must be greater than or equal to the number of bytes in the next parameter.

GetTickCountLow.summary: Get the number of 1/10000 seconds that have elapsed since Windows was started.

Globals.summary: Support class to support runtime access to globals declared in the loaded X# assemblies

Globals.Find.summary: Find the Global Field with a name
Globals.Find.param: Name of the global to find

Globals.Get.summary: Return the value of a Global
Globals.Get.param: Name of the global to find
Globals.Get.returns: TRUE when a global with the name exists, FALSE when it does not exist

Globals.GetAllGlobals.summary: Return a list of all Global Fields. This is used in the Globals Debugger Window

Globals.Put.summary: Assign a value to a Global
Globals.Put.param: Name of the global to assign
Globals.Put.returns: TRUE when a global with the name exists, and the value was successfully assigned. FALSE when it does not exist or when there was an error assigning the value.,

IDynamicProperties.summary: This interface is used for objects that implement a special mechanism for reading / writing properties at runtime.

IDynamicProperties.GetPropertyNames.summary: Retrieve an array of the property names that exist.

IDynamicProperties.NoIvarGet.summary: Retrieve the value of a property by name.
IDynamicProperties.NoIvarGet.returns: The current value of the property
IDynamicProperties.NoIvarGet.remarks: Accessing a property that does not exist will result in a runtime error.

IDynamicProperties.NoIvarPut.summary: Update the value of a property by name.
IDynamicProperties.NoIvarPut.remarks:
Assigning a property that does not exist may result in a runtime error.
However the class may also decide to "silently" add the property.

IIndexedProperties.summary: This interface can be used to access any object with an indexer. This is mostly used for elements inside typed arrays of the 'ARRAY OF' class. If you implement the interface on the elements you can use an array syntax to assess fields/properties in the elements of the array by name or ordinal.

IIndexedProperties.self_name.summary: Get/Set array elements with a name.
IIndexedProperties.self_name.param: Name which will be used to lookup a property

IIndexer.summary: This interface is used to index a collection using the VO Array syntax. The interface is implemented by the ARRAY type in the runtime, but you can also use it for your custom types.

ILateBound.summary:
This interface described the methods needed for a LateBound object
When an object implements this interface then the X# runtime will not use reflection to implement late bound calls to properties or methods but will call these methods instead.

ILateBound.NoIvarGet.summary: Method to read a field or property late bound.

ILateBound.NoIvarPut.summary: Method to update a field or property late bound.

ILateBound.NoMethod.summary: Call a method in the type late bound. Arguments are passed as Clipper calling convention parameters. The method name is passed as first parameter.

IMacroCompilerUsual.summary: This interface defines and extension to the Macro compiler subsystem

IMacroCompilerUsual.CompileCodeblock.summary: Compile a string into a runtime codeblock.
IMacroCompilerUsual.CompileCodeblock.param: will be set to TRUE when the string was a real codeblock (with {|..| }).
will be set to TRUE when the macro contains code that may result in adding new MemVars).

INamedIndexer.summary: This interface is used to index a collection using a numeric and a string index and is implemented by the typed array class ('ARRAY OF'). If your elements inside the collection implement the IIndexProperties interface then the lookup of the property inside array element will be resolved with a call to the named indexer on that object.

InList.remarks: InList() determines whether an expression is found in a series of expressions. The first expression is compared to each of the other expressions until either a match is found or the entire list has been traversed. For exact matching, use InListExact().

InListExact.remarks: InListExact() determines whether an expression is found in a series of expressions. The first expression is compared to each of the other expressions until either a match is found or the entire list has been traversed. InListExact() is the same as InList() except that == is used for matching instead of =. There is no difference between InList() and InListExact() for non string datatypes.
InListExact.example:
This example compares InList() and InListExact() when the left operand in a comparison is longer than the right operand of a comparison:
? InList("longer", "long") // TRUE ? InList("long ", "long") // TRUE ? InListExact("longer", "long") // FALSE ? InListExact("long ", "long") // FALSE
This example compares InList() and InListExact() when the right operand in a comparison is longer than the left operand of a comparison:
? InList("long", "longer") // FALSE ? InList("long", "long ") // FALSE ? InListExact("long", "longer") // FALSE ? InListExact("long", "long ") // FALSE

IRtCodeblock.summary: This interface Extended the ICodeblock interface and adds support for usual parameters and return values

IRtCodeblock.Eval.param: Zero or more arguments to pass to the codeblock.
IRtCodeblock.Eval.returns: The value of the last expression in the codeblock.

IRtCodeblock.IsBlock.summary: Was the codeblock created from a string that started with "{|"

IsAlNum.summary: Determine if the leftmost character in a PSZ is alphanumeric.
IsAlNum.returns: TRUE if the first character is either alphabetic or numeric otherwise FALSE.

IsAlpha.summary: Determine if the leftmost character in a PSZ is alphabetic.
IsAlpha.returns: TRUE if the first character is alphabetic.

IsAlphaNum.summary: Determine if the leftmost character in a PSZ is alphanumeric.
IsAlphaNum.returns: TRUE if the first character is alphanumeric.

IsBDigit.summary: Determine if the leftmost character in a PSZ is a binary digit (0 or 1)).
IsBDigit.returns: TRUE if the first character of the string is 0 or 1 otherwise FALSE.

IsBinary.summary: Determine if a value is a Binary.
IsBinary.returns: TRUE if the value is a Binary data type; otherwise, FALSE.

IsByRef.summary: Determine if a value is passed by reference

IsCurrency.summary: Determine if a value is a Currency.
IsCurrency.returns: TRUE if the value is a Currency data type; otherwise, FALSE.

IsDateTime.summary: Determine if a value is a DateTime.
IsDateTime.returns: TRUE if the value is a DATETIME data type; otherwise, FALSE.

IsDecimal.summary: Determine if a value is a Decimal.
IsDecimal.returns: TRUE if the value is a Decimal data type; otherwise, FALSE.

IsFractional.summary: Determine if a value is a Decimal or a Float
IsFractional.returns: TRUE if the value is a Decimal, Currency or Float data type; otherwise, FALSE.

IsInt64.summary: Determine if a value is a INT64.
IsInt64.returns: TRUE if the value is a INT64 data type; otherwise, FALSE.

IsInteger.summary: Determine if a value is an integer (LONG or INT64).
IsInteger.returns: TRUE if the value is a LONG or INT64 data type; otherwise, FALSE.

IsLower.summary: Determine if the leftmost character in a PSZ is lower.
IsLower.returns: TRUE if the first character is a lowercase letter otherwise, FALSE.

IsNumeric.returns: TRUE if the value is a LONG, FLOAT, IN64 or DECIMAL data type; otherwise, FALSE.

IsSpace.summary: Determine if the leftmost character in a PSZ is a blank (that is, Chr(9) through Chr(13) or Chr(32)).
IsSpace.returns: TRUE if the first character of the string blank otherwise FALSE.

IsUpper.summary: Determine if the leftmost character in a PSZ is uppercase.
IsUpper.returns: TRUE if the first character is an uppercase letter otherwise, FALSE.

IsXDigit.summary: Determine if the leftmost character in a PSZ is a hex character (that is, digits from 1 through 9 and letters from A through F).
IsXDigit.returns: TRUE if the first character is hex otherwise FALSE.

MaxAtom.summary: Determine the number of Symbols in the atom table.

MCompile.remarks: MCompile() allows you to use the macro compiler to compile a string and store the compiled results for later execution. Instead of invoking the macro compiler each time an expression is evaluated, you could speed up your application by compiling an expression only once and executing the compiled form as often as desired.
MCompile.param: Should single quotes be allowed as string delimiters
MCompile.note: MCompile returns a STRING in VO. It returns a XSharp._Codeblock in .Net.

MemGrpCAlloc.param: The group to which the newly allocated memory buffer will belong. This group should have already been opened by MemGrpOpen().

MemGrpEnum.summary: Enumerate all the pointers allocated in a memory group
MemGrpEnum.param: The group you want to compact
MemGrpEnum.returns: TRUE when all delegate calls return TRUE

MemRealloc.summary: ReAllocate a static memory buffer of a specified size.
MemRealloc.returns:
Returns the original pointer when the nSize parameter is smaller or equal to the current size.
Extraneous bytes are zeroed out. Returns a new buffer when the requesed size is bigger than the original size.

MemVar.summary: Internal type that implements the Dynamic Memory Variables.

MemVar._Get.summary: Get the value of a local, private or public (in that order). Throws an exception when the variable does not exist.
MemVar._Get.returns: The value of the memory variable.
MemVar._Get.param: The name of the memory variable

MemVar._GetSafe.summary: Get the value of a local, private or public (in that order). Returns NIL if the value does not exist.
MemVar._GetSafe.returns: The value of the memory variable or NIL when it does not exist.
MemVar._GetSafe.param: The name of the memory variable
MemVar._GetSafe.remarks: When a private and a public exist with the same name then the private has preference

MemVar._Put.summary: Updates the value of a local, private or public (in that order). If the value does not exist than a new variable at the current level is created.
MemVar._Put.returns: The value of the memory variable.
MemVar._Put.param: The name of the memory variable.

MemVar.Add.summary: Add a public memvar or a private memvar to the current level.
MemVar.Add.param: The name of the memory variable
Should the variable be created as private

MemVar.Clear.summary: Clear a variable by name. Tries to clear a private first and when that is not found then a public
MemVar.Clear.param: The name of the memory variable.
MemVar.Clear.remarks:
Clear does not remove the variable but sets its value to NIL.
In the FoxPro dialect Clear does not clear local variables when these are visible.

MemVar.ClearAll.summary: Clear all memvar name/value pairs. Does not remove locals. Does not remove privates stack levels.

MemVar.GetHigherLevelPrivate.summary: Get a memvar object from the stack (if it exists)

MemVar.Getter.param: Name of the Memvar for which the value has to be retrieved
MemVar.Getter.returns: The value of the memory variable.

MemVar.PrivateFind.summary: Find a private variable. Try on the current level on the stack first and when not found then walk the stack.

MemVar.PrivatePut.summary: Update a private variable. Does NOT add a new variable

MemVar.PrivatesCount.summary: Get the total number of unique private variable names.

MemVar.PrivatesEnum.summary: Get an enumerator for all the unique names of private variables

MemVar.PrivatesFirst.summary: Get the first unique private variable name.

MemVar.PrivatesNext.summary: Get the next unique private variable name.

MemVar.PublicFind.param: The name of the memory variable.

MemVar.PublicPut.summary: Update a public variable. Does NOT create a new public when there is no variable with that name.
MemVar.PublicPut.param: The name of the memory variable.

MemVar.PublicsCount.summary: Gets the total number of public variables.

MemVar.PublicsEnum.summary: Get an enumerator for all the unique names of public variables

MemVar.PublicsFirst.summary: Gets the name of the first public variable.

MemVar.PublicsNext.summary: Gets the name of the next public variable.

MemVar.Putter.param: Name of the Memvar for which the value has to be retrieved
Value to assign to the memory variable
MemVar.Putter.remarks: When the variable does not exist then a new memory variable may be created.

MemVar.ReleaseAll.summary: Assign NIL to all visible private variables. Hidden privates are not affected.

MemVar.ReleasePrivates.summary: Release all privates at a certain level and higher

MemVar.TryGet.summary: Try to retrieve the value of a local, private or public (in that order).
MemVar.TryGet.param: The name of the memory variable
Value of the variable, or NIL whe the variable does not exist.
MemVar.TryGet.returns: TRUE when the variable was found. Otherwise FALSE

MemVar.Value.summary: Value of the memory variable. The default is NIL for PRIVATEs and FALSE for PUBLICs.

MemVarClear.param: The name of the variable you want to clear.
MemVarClear.remarks: The value of this variable will be set to NIL. The variable is NOT deleted.

MemVarRelease.param: The name of the variable you want to release.

MemWalk.remarks: Only memory blocks that were allocated while MemTrace was set to TRUE will be included.

MemWalker.summary: Delegate that a client needs to implement to use MemWalk.

Object2Float.summary: Convert an object containing a numeric value to a FLOAT
Object2Float.param: Object containing the numeric value to convert.
Object2Float.returns: The value in the form of a float.
Object2Float.exception: Thrown when the parameter oValue cannot be converted to a FLOAT.

OOPHelpers.ConvertFromNull.summary: Convert a null value to the correct value for the type

OOPHelpers.CountNonDefaultParameters.summary: Count the number of parameters that do not have a default value

OOPHelpers.IsInternalVisible.summary: This method returns TRUE when the assembly from which an IVarGet() or IVarPut() was called is the same assembly in which a property was defined.
OOPHelpers.IsInternalVisible.returns: TRUE when the first stackframe outside of XSharp.RT is in the same assembly as

OrdCount.summary: Determines the number of orders for the current work area.
OrdCount.returns: OrdCount() returns the number of open indexes as a numeric value. When no index is open or when no file is open in the current workarea, the return value is 0.

OrdList.summary: Return a list of all tag names for the current work area.
OrdList.returns: OrdList() returns a one dimensional array holding strings with the tag names of all open indexes. When no index is open, an empty array is returned.

Set.summary: Changes and/or reads a system setting.
Set.param: Is a positive integer identifying a system setting or system variable. This should match the values from the Set enumerated type.
The optional expression can specify a new value for a system setting. The data type is dependent on the system setting designated by .
Set.returns: When Set() is called without the argument , the function returns the current system setting designated by . If is specified, the corresponding system setting is set to and the value of the old setting is returned.
Set.remarks: If you are coming from XHarbour or Xbase++ please don't use set.ch for the value of because there are some differences between the values in this header file and the values used inside X#.

ShowArray.summary: Dump the contents of an array to the terminal window
ShowArray.param: Name to show before the array brackets. Defaults to 'a'
ShowArray.remarks: This dumps the information to the terminal window. This will only work if the main application is a console application.

ShowObject.summary: Dump the contents of an object to the terminal window
ShowObject.param: Name to show before the field names. Defaults to 'o'
ShowObject.remarks: This dumps the information to the terminal window. This will only work if the main application is a console application.

ShowPrivates.summary: Dump the currently defined privates to the terminal window
ShowPrivates.param: Only dump the privates from the current level on the evaluation stack.
ShowPrivates.remarks: This dumps the information to the terminal window. This will only work if the main application is a console application.

ShowPublics.summary: Dump the currently defined publics to the terminal window
ShowPublics.remarks: This dumps the information to the terminal window. This will only work if the main application is a console application.

Str2.returns: A string representation of the value.

String2Atom.summary: Convert a string to a Symbol.

String2Symbol.summary: Convert a string to an uppercase Symbol.

SysAddAtom.summary: Convert a string to a Symbol and add it to the atom table.

SysAddAtomUpperA.summary: Convert a string to an uppercase Symbol and add it to the atom table.

SysFindAtom.summary: Determine whether a Symbol is in the atom table.

SysGetAtomName.summary: Convert a Symbol to a null-terminated string.

UnRegisterAxit.summary: Terminate the registration of an object that has been registered with RegisterAxit().

UsualType.summary: Determine the data type of an expression.

UsualVal.summary: Access contents of an address, whether it is passed by reference or not.

ValType.summary: Determine the data type of an expression.

Version.summary: Returns the version of X# you are using.

VoDb.summary:
The VoDb class extends the CoreDb class with methods that take usual parameters or return usual values.
All other methods are identical and inherited from the CoreDb class.

remarks.note: The difference between VoDb.Info and CoreDb.Info is that VoDb.Info takes a USUAL parameter
The difference between VoDb.OrderInfo and CoreDb.OrderInfo is that VoDb.Info takes a USUAL parameter
The difference between VoDb.RddInfo and CoreDb.RddInfo is that VoDb.RddInfo takes a USUAL parameter
The difference between VoDb.RecordInfo and CoreDb.RecordInfo is that VoDb.RecordInfo takes a USUAL parameter
The difference between VoDb.Select and CoreDb.Select is that VoDb.Select takes a USUAL parameter
The difference between VoDb.SetFilter and CoreDb.SetFilter is that VoDb.SetFilter takes a USUAL parameter

doc.VoDbAlias: These functions all map to the static methods inside VoDb (and CoreDb)

remarks.note: VoDbCloseArea() is an alias for CoreDbCloseArea()

VoDbCreate.param: Array with structure to use when creating the file.
Type of the RDDs to use when creating the file
structure that describes the list of RDDs to use

remarks.note: The difference between VoDbCreate and CoreDb.Create is that VoDbCreate takes a ARRAY parameter

VoDbFieldInfo.param: New value to assign to the Field.

VoDbOrderInfo.param: New value to assign to the Order.

VoDbOrdSetFocus.param: This returns the previous order that was selected.

remarks.note: The difference between VoDbRddInfo and CoreDb.RddInfo is that VoDbRddInfo takes a USUAL parameter

VoDbSort.param: List of field names to copy
List of field names to sort on

VoDbTrans.param: List of field names to copy

remarks.note: The difference between VoDbUnlock and CoreDb.UnLock is that VoDbUnlock takes USUAL parameters

VoDbUseArea.param: Name of the RDD to use.
Type of the RDD to use.

XSharpLoadLibrary.summary: Dynamically loads a library (dll) compiled with X#, running any _INIT procedures it may contain.
XSharpLoadLibrary.param: The full path of the library to load.
XSharpLoadLibrary.returns: The Assembly object of the loaded library.
